req.title: Inter-facility referral
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three verifiable behaviours: (1) a valid referral is stored and a secure message dispatched (happy path, EP), (2) a referral without patient consent is rejected with HTTP 403 (NEG/DT), and (3) a referral payload at the minimum viable boundary—exactly one required field per FHIR ReferralRequest—is accepted (BVA). An additional State Transition test covers the referral lifecycle from 'draft' through 'active' to verify the trigger causes the correct state change. Each test authenticates as a clinician, posts to /ReferralRequest, and asserts HTTP status plus response body fields. Fixtures supply auth tokens and base URL; helpers reset state between runs to ensure idempotency.

tests.name: test_valid_referral_stored_and_message_dispatched
preconditions: Patient consent record exists for the target facility
Target facility is registered in the system
actions: Build a well-formed ReferralRequest payload with all required FHIR fields
POST payload to /ReferralRequest with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Secure message dispatched: message queue or audit log records the event
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_valid_referral_stored_and_message_dispatched(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    consent_present_patient, # <fixture> returns patient_id with consent
    target_facility_id,      # <fixture>
    assert_message_dispatched # <helper_fn>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{consent_present_patient}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Organization/{target_facility_id}"}],
        "requester": {"agent": {"reference": "Practitioner/clinician-001"}}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Referral ID missing from response"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert_message_dispatched(body["id"])  # <helper_fn> polls audit/message queue

tests.name: test_referral_rejected_when_consent_absent
preconditions: Patient has NO consent record for the target facility
Target facility is registered in the system
actions: Build a ReferralRequest payload referencing a patient without consent
POST payload to /ReferralRequest with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating consent absent
No referral record created in the datastore
trace: R-HLT-017: error pathway — 403 when consent absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_rejected_when_consent_absent(
    clinician_auth_headers,     # <fixture>
    base_url,                   # <fixture>
    no_consent_patient,         # <fixture> returns patient_id WITHOUT consent
    target_facility_id,         # <fixture>
    assert_no_record_created,   # <helper_fn>
    assert_no_message_dispatched # <helper_fn>
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{no_consent_patient}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Organization/{target_facility_id}"}],
        "requester": {"agent": {"reference": "Practitioner/clinician-001"}}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "consent" in str(body).lower(), "Error body should mention consent"
    assert_no_record_created(no_consent_patient)   # <helper_fn>
    assert_no_message_dispatched(no_consent_patient) # <helper_fn>

tests.name: test_referral_with_minimum_required_fields_accepted
preconditions: Patient consent record exists for the target facility
Payload contains exactly the FHIR-minimum required fields (no optional fields)
actions: Build a ReferralRequest with only mandatory FHIR R4 fields: resourceType, status, subject
POST minimal payload to /ReferralRequest with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Referral is stored with default values for omitted optional fields
Secure message dispatched for the minimal referral
trace: R-HLT-017: boundary — minimum viable payload accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_with_minimum_required_fields_accepted(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    consent_present_patient, # <fixture>
    assert_message_dispatched # <helper_fn>
):
    # BVA: exactly the lower boundary of required fields — no optional fields included
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{consent_present_patient}"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Minimal payload should be accepted; got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must include assigned referral ID"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert_message_dispatched(body["id"])  # <helper_fn>

tests.name: test_referral_state_transitions_draft_to_active
preconditions: Patient consent record exists for the target facility
A draft ReferralRequest exists for the patient
actions: Retrieve the draft referral to confirm initial state is 'draft'
PUT/PATCH the referral status to 'active' (submit/send action)
Capture HTTP response and confirm state changed to 'active'
Verify secure message is dispatched only after transition to 'active'
expected: Secure message dispatched after state becomes 'active'
trace: R-HLT-017: clinician sends referral (trigger causes state change)
R-HLT-017: secure message dispatched on active state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_state_transitions_draft_to_active(
    clinician_auth_headers,  # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    draft_referral_id,       # <fixture> pre-creates a draft ReferralRequest, returns id
    assert_message_dispatched # <helper_fn>
):
    # STC: verify draft -> active transition and side-effect
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest/{draft_referral_id}",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "draft", "Precondition: must start as draft"

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest/{draft_referral_id}",
        json={"status": "active"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 200, f"PATCH failed: {patch_resp.text}"

    confirm_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest/{draft_referral_id}",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert confirm_resp.json().get("status") == "active"
    assert_message_dispatched(draft_referral_id)  # <helper_fn>

tests.name: test_referral_decision_table_consent_role_combinations
preconditions: System has users with roles: clinician and non-clinician (e.g. patient role)
Both consent-present and consent-absent patient records exist
actions: For each combination of (role, consent) attempt POST to /ReferralRequest
Assert outcome matches decision table: only clinician+consent yields 201
trace: R-HLT-017: error pathway — 403 when consent absent
R-HLT-017: decision table covering role x consent matrix
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("auth_fixture,patient_fixture,expected_status", [
    ("clinician_auth_headers", "consent_present_patient", 201),
    ("clinician_auth_headers", "no_consent_patient",      403),
    ("non_clinician_auth_headers", "consent_present_patient", 403),
])
def test_referral_decision_table_consent_role_combinations(
    request,
    auth_fixture,
    patient_fixture,
    expected_status,
    base_url,          # <fixture>
    target_facility_id # <fixture>
):
    headers  = request.getfixturevalue(auth_fixture)   # <fixture>
    patient  = request.getfixturevalue(patient_fixture) # <fixture>
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Organization/{target_facility_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"[{auth_fixture}+{patient_fixture}] Expected {expected_status}, "
        f"got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
